TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua azua vatu a nakitjuqizing, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: izua azua vatu a nakicuqiziŋ, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: izua azua vatu a nakitjuqizing, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: izua azua vatu a nakiʦuqiziŋ, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: There were some dogs which had belonged to Qizing village (OA; or which had been usurped), according to the story.

S.FORM: izua i Paliɫaliɫaw a vatu.
S.PHON: izua i paɭiʎaɭiʎaw a vatu.
S.FORM: izua i Paliljaliljaw a vatu.
S.PHON: izua i paɣiʎaɣiʎaw a vatu.
S.TRANSL: There were dogs in the Lower Paiwan area.

S.FORM: saka nu qemaɫup, nu qaqatsaqatsaan a vavuy a venan a takets, lingeɫen nua vatu a pasa dudut tua qinaɫan; sa djadjasi sa kani a mapuɫat.
S.PHON: saka nu qəmaʎup, nu qaqaʦaqaʦaan a vavuj a vənan a takəʦ, ɭiŋəʎən nua vatu a pasa dudut tua qinaʎan; sa ɉaɉasi sa kani a mapuʎat.
S.FORM: saka nu qemaljup, nu qaqacaqacaan a vavuy a venan a takec, lingeljen nua vatu a pasa dudut tua qinaljan; sa djadjasi sa kani a mapuljat.
S.PHON: saka nu qəmaʎup, nu qaqaʦaqaʦaan a vavuj a vənan a takəʦ, ɣiŋəʎən nua vatu a pasa dudut tua qinaʎan; sa ɉaɉasi sa kani a mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: When there was hunting, the big wild pigs, deer and muntjacs were surrounded and driven by the dogs to near the village, and were all caught and eaten.

S.FORM: azua nu kasi tsadja, inika makakatsu tu qaqatsaqatsaan.
S.PHON: azua nu kasi ʦaɉa, inika makakaʦu tu qaqaʦaqaʦaan.
S.FORM: azua nu kasi cadja, inika makakacu tu qaqacaqacaan.
S.PHON: azua nu kasi ʦaɉa, inika makakaʦu tu qaqaʦaqaʦaan.
S.TRANSL: When they came from a distance, they couldn’t carry the big ones;

S.FORM: avan nu sika lemingeɫ a pasa dudut tua tapaw.
S.PHON: avan nu sika ɭəmiŋəʎ a pasa dudut tua tapaw.
S.FORM: avan nu sika lemingelj a pasa dudut tua tapaw.
S.PHON: avan nu sika ɣəmiŋəʎ a pasa dudut tua tapaw.
S.TRANSL: that’s why they drove them to near the houses.

S.FORM: avan a kakeḍikeḍian a tangpatsen a sema tapaw, sa kani a mapuɫat.
S.PHON: avan a kakəɖikəɖian a taŋpaʦən a səma tapaw, sa kani a mapuʎat.
S.FORM: avan a kakedrikedrian a tangpacen a sema tapaw, sa kani a mapuljat.
S.PHON: avan a kakəɖikəɖian a taŋpaʦən a səma tapaw, sa kani a mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: The little ones were carried in the mouth to the houses, and were all eaten.

S.FORM: manu sema pairang a tsautsau a maḍusa mareqali.
S.PHON: manu səma pairaŋ a ʦauʦau a maɖusa marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: manu sema pairang a caucau a madrusa mareqali.
S.PHON: manu səma paiRaŋ a ʦauʦau a maɖusa maRəqaɣi.
S.TRANSL: Two men friends went down to the plains.

S.FORM: manu djumaken nazua vatu, sa katsi, sa patsay azua tsautsau a maḍusa.
S.PHON: manu ɉumakən nazua vatu, sa kaʦi, sa paʦaj azua ʦauʦau a maɖusa.
S.FORM: manu djumaken nazua vatu, sa kaci, sa pacay azua caucau a madrusa.
S.PHON: manu ɉumakən nazua vatu, sa kaʦi, sa paʦaj azua ʦauʦau a maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: Some dogs found them and bit them, and both men died.

S.FORM: "aku izua zazua vatu? saɫinga aravats," aya a tsautsau.
S.PHON: "aku izua zazua vatuʔ saʎiŋa aravaʦ," aja a ʦauʦau.
S.FORM: "aku izua zazua vatu' saljinga aravac," aya a caucau.
S.PHON: "aku izua zazua vatuʔ saʎiŋa aRavaʦ," aja a ʦauʦau.
S.TRANSL: People said: “Why are there dogs like that? We would very much like some.”

S.FORM: saka kemats azua.
S.PHON: saka kəmaʦ azua.
S.FORM: saka kemac azua.
S.PHON: saka kəmaʦ azua.

S.FORM: manu "tja kudain anga," aya.
S.PHON: manu "ca kudain aŋa," aja.
S.FORM: manu "tja kudain anga," aya.
S.PHON: manu "ʦa kudain aŋa," aja.
S.TRANSL: So they said: “What shall we do?”

S.FORM: saka qemavay, sa paluvluvi tua quvaɫ.
S.PHON: saka qəmavaj, sa paɭuvɭuvi tua quvaʎ.
S.FORM: saka qemavay, sa paluvluvi tua quvalj.
S.PHON: saka qəmavaj, sa paɣuvɣuvi tua quvaʎ.
S.TRANSL: They made dumplings, and mixed hair in with them.

S.FORM: saka keman a vatu, sa katsingas a aɫis nua vatu.
S.PHON: saka kəman a vatu, sa kaʦiŋas a aʎis nua vatu.
S.FORM: saka keman a vatu, sa kacingas a aljis nua vatu.
S.PHON: saka kəman a vatu, sa kaʦiŋas a aʎis nua vatu.
S.TRANSL: And when the dogs ate them, their teeth were all stuck up.

S.FORM: sa djekepi nua tsautsau a kuku a ḍusa, a uqaɫay a ita, a vavayan a ita; sa vaikan anga a sema a paqaluqalu.
S.PHON: sa ɉəkəpi nua ʦauʦau a kuku a ɖusa, a uqaʎaj a ita, a vavajan a ita; sa vaikan aŋa a səma a paqaɭuqaɭu.
S.FORM: sa djekepi nua caucau a kuku a drusa, a uqaljay a ita, a vavayan a ita; sa vaikan anga a sema a paqaluqalu.
S.PHON: sa ɉəkəpi nua ʦauʦau a kuku a ɖusa, a uqaʎaj a ita, a vavajan a ita; sa vaikan aŋa a səma a paqaɣuqaɣu.
S.TRANSL: So the people took two puppies, one male and one female; and they took them off to the East Paiwan area.

S.FORM: sa puaɫak anga, sa puvatu anga a tsautsau, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: sa puaʎak aŋa, sa puvatu aŋa a ʦauʦau, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: sa pualjak anga, sa puvatu anga a caucau, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: sa puaʎak aŋa, sa puvatu aŋa a ʦauʦau, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: The dogs had puppies, and so people got dogs, according to the story.
